name: podman
description: Manage containers, pods, and images with Podman

subcommands.name: attach
subcommands.description: Attach to a running container

options.description: Override the key sequence for detaching a container
Do not attach STDIN
Proxy all received signals to the process (default true)

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Build an image using instructions from Containerfiles

options.description: Skip image verification (default true)
Name of the Dockerfile (Default is 'PATH/Dockerfile')
Write the image ID to the file
Container isolation technology
Set metadata for an image
Set the networking mode for the RUN instructions during build (default "default")
Do not use cache when building the image
Output destination (format: type=local,dest=path)
Set platform if server is multi-platform capable
Set type of progress output (auto, plain, tty). Use plain to show container output
Always attempt to pull a newer version of the image
Suppress the build output and print image ID on success
Secret file to expose to the build (only if BuildKit enabled): id=mysecret,src=/local/secret
Squash newly built layers into a single new layer
SSH agent socket or keys to expose to the build (only if BuildKit enabled) (format: default|<id>[=<socket>|<key>[,<key>]])
Name and optionally a tag in the 'name:tag' format
Set the target build stage to build

subcommands.name: commit
subcommands.description: Create new image based on the changed container

options.description: Author (e.g., "John Hannibal Smith <hannibal@a-team.com>")
Apply Dockerfile instruction to the created image
Commit message
Pause container during commit (default true)

subcommands.name: cp
subcommands.description: Copy files/folders between a container and the local filesystem

options.description: Archive mode (copy all uid/gid information)
Always follow symbol link in SRC_PATH

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create but do not start a container

options.description: Add a custom host-to-IP mapping (host:ip)
Attach to STDIN, STDOUT or STDERR
Block IO (relative weight), between 10 and 1000, or 0 to disable (default 0)
Block IO weight (relative device weight) (default [])
Add Linux capabilities
Drop Linux capabilities
Optional parent cgroup for the container
Cgroup namespace to use (host|private)
Write the container ID to the file
Limit CPU CFS (Completely Fair Scheduler) period
Limit CPU CFS (Completely Fair Scheduler) quota
Limit CPU real-time period in microseconds
Limit CPU real-time runtime in microseconds
CPU shares (relative weight)
Number of CPUs
CPUs in which to allow execution (0-3, 0,1)
MEMs in which to allow execution (0-3, 0,1)
Add a host device to the container
Add a rule to the cgroup allowed devices list
Limit read rate (bytes per second) from a device (default [])
Limit read rate (IO per second) from a device (default [])
Limit write rate (bytes per second) to a device (default [])
Limit write rate (IO per second) to a device (default [])
Skip image verification (default true)
Set custom DNS servers
Set DNS options
Set custom DNS search domains
Container NIS domain name
Overwrite the default ENTRYPOINT of the image
Set environment variables
Read in a file of environment variables
Expose a port or a range of ports
GPU devices to add to the container ('all' to pass all GPUs)
Add additional groups to join
Command to run to check health
Time between running the check (ms|s|m|h) (default 0s)
Consecutive failures needed to report unhealthy
Start period for the container to initialize before starting health-retries countdown (ms|s|m|h) (default 0s)
Maximum time to allow one check to run (ms|s|m|h) (default 0s)
Print usage
Container host name
Run an init inside the container that forwards signals and reaps processes
Keep STDIN open even if not attached
IPv6 address (e.g., 2001:db8::33)
IPC mode to use

Kernel memory limit
Set meta data on a container
Read in a line delimited file of labels

Tune container memory swappiness (0 to 100) (default -1)

Assign a name to the container
Connect a container to a network

Disable any container-specified HEALTHCHECK
Disable OOM Killer

Give extended privileges to this container
Publish a container's port(s) to the host
Publish all exposed ports to random ports
Pull image before creating ("always"|"missing"|"never") (default "missing")
Mount the container's root filesystem as read only
Restart policy to apply when a container exits (default "no")
Automatically remove the container when it exits
Runtime to use for this container
Security Options
Size of /dev/shm
Signal to stop a container (default "SIGTERM")
Timeout (in seconds) to stop a container
Storage driver options for the container

Mount a tmpfs directory
Allocate a pseudo-TTY
Ulimit options (default [])
Username or UID (format: <name|uid>[:<group|gid>])

UTS namespace to use
Bind mount a volume
Optional volume driver for the container
Mount volumes from the specified container(s)
Working directory inside the container

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: Display the changes to the object's file system

subcommands.name: events
subcommands.description: Show podman system events

options.description: Filter output based on conditions provided
Format the output using the given Go template
Show all events created since timestamp
Stream events until this timestamp

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Run a process in a running container

options.description: Launch an interactive session
Detached mode: run command in the background
Override the key sequence for detaching a container
Set environment variables
Read in a file of environment variables
Keep STDIN open even if not attached
Give extended privileges to the command
Allocate a pseudo-TTY
Username or UID (format: <name|uid>[:<group|gid>])
Working directory inside the container

subcommands.name: export
subcommands.description: Export container's filesystem contents as a tar archive

options.description: Write to a file, instead of STDOUT

subcommands.name: history
subcommands.description: Show history of a specified image

options.description: Pretty-print images using a Go template
Print sizes and dates in human readable format (default true)
Don't truncate output
Only show image IDs

subcommands.name: images
subcommands.description: List images in local storage

options.description: Show all images (default hides intermediate images)
Show digests
Filter output based on conditions provided
Pretty-print images using a Go template
Don't truncate output
Only show image IDs

subcommands.name: import
subcommands.description: Import a tarball to create a filesystem image

options.description: Apply Dockerfile instruction to the created image
Set commit message for imported image
Set platform if server is multi-platform capable

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Display podman system information

options.description: Format the output using the given Go template

subcommands.name: inspect
subcommands.description: Display the configuration of object denoted by ID

options.description: Format the output using the given Go template
Display total file sizes if the type is container
Return JSON for specified type

subcommands.name: kill
subcommands.description: Kill one or more running containers with a specific signal

options.description: Signal to send to the container (default "KILL")

subcommands.name: load
subcommands.description: Load image(s) from a tar archive

options.description: Read from tar archive file, instead of STDIN
Suppress the load output

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: Log in to a container registry

options.description: Password
Take the password from stdin
Username

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: Log out of a container registry

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: Fetch the logs of one or more containers

options.description: Show extra details provided to logs
Follow log output
Show logs since timestamp (e.g. 2013-01-02T13:23:37Z) or relative (e.g. 42m for 42 minutes)
Number of lines to show from the end of the logs (default "all")
Show timestamps
Show logs before a timestamp (e.g. 2013-01-02T13:23:37Z) or relative (e.g. 42m for 42 minutes)

subcommands.name: pause
subcommands.description: Pause all processes within one or more containers

subcommands.name: port
subcommands.description: List port mappings or a specific mapping for the container

subcommands.name: ps
subcommands.description: List containers

options.description: Show all containers (default shows just running)
Filter output based on conditions provided
Pretty-print containers using a Go template
Show n last created containers (includes all states) (default -1)
Show the latest created container (includes all states)
Don't truncate output
Only display container IDs
Display total file sizes

subcommands.name: pull
subcommands.description: Pull an image from a registry

options.description: Download all tagged images in the repository
Skip image verification (default true)
Set platform if server is multi-platform capable
Suppress verbose output

subcommands.name: push
subcommands.description: Push an image to a specified destination

options.description: Push all tagged images in the repository
Skip image signing (default true)
Suppress verbose output

subcommands.name: rename
subcommands.description: Rename an existing container

subcommands.name: restart
subcommands.description: Restart one or more containers

options.description: Seconds to wait for stop before killing the container (default 10)

subcommands.name: rm
subcommands.description: Remove one or more containers

options.description: Force the removal of a running container (uses SIGKILL)
Remove the specified link
Remove the anonymous volumes associated with the container

subcommands.name: rmi
subcommands.description: Remove one or more images from local storage

options.description: Force removal of the image
Do not delete untagged parents
